• Zoé Pomarat

    Zoé Pomarat

    Postdoctoral researcher - LAAS-CNRS

    • Toulouse, France
    • LAAS-CNRS
    • Email
    • Google Scholar

    About

    I am currently a postdoctoral researcher at LAAS-CNRS, Toulouse, in the Gepetto team. My research focuses on sports biomechanics and applied machine learning, with an emphasis on force estimation and instrumentation in elite sport contexts. I work at the intersection of experimental biomechanics, sensor design, and data-driven methods, developing custom ML models tailored to the specific constraints of real-world sport environments.

    During my PhD, I focused on rugby scrum instrumentation and individual force estimation using instrumented insoles and machine learning, in collaboration with Stade Toulousain and Institut Clément Ader, supervised by Prof. Bruno Watier, Prof. Jean-Charles Passieux, and Asst. Prof. John-Eric Dufour.

    I graduated from INSA Toulouse, where I specialised in mechanical engineering and biomechanics.

    Publications

    • 2025: Machine learning techniques for estimating the individual three-dimensional ground reaction forces during rugby scrummaging. Zoé Pomarat, Jean-Charles Passieux, John-Eric Dufour, Bruno Watier. Published in Journal of Biomechanics, 2025.

    • 2025: Wheelchair caster power losses due to rolling resistance on sports surfaces. Zoé Pomarat, Thibault Marsan, Arnaud Faupin, Yann Landon, Bruno Watier. Published in Disability and Rehabilitation: Assistive Technology, 2025.

    • 2024: Estimation of Ground Reaction Forces in Rugby Scrummaging Using Instrumented Insoles and Machine Learning. Zoé Pomarat, Kahina Chalabi, Maxime Sabbah, John-Eric Dufour, Jean-Charles Passieux, Bruno Watier. Published at 19th International Symposium on 3D Analysis of Human Movement (3DAHM), Montevideo, Uruguay.

    • 2024: Ground Reaction Forces and Moments Estimation from Embedded Insoles using Machine Learning Regression Models. Maxime Sabbah, Raphaël Dumas, Zoé Pomarat, Lucas Robinet, Mohamed Adjel, Bruno Watier, Vincent Bonnet. Published at IEEE BioRob 2024.

    • 2023: Kinetics influence of multibody kinematics optimisation for soft tissue artefact compensation. Zoé Pomarat, Sacha Guitteny, Raphaël Dumas, Antoine Muller. Published in Journal of Biomechanics, 2023.

    • 2022: Influence of multibody kinematics optimisation on knee intersegmental moments assessment. Zoé Pomarat, Sacha Guitteny, Raphaël Dumas, Antoine Muller. Published at 17th International Symposium on 3D Analysis of Human Movement (3DAHM), Tokyo, Japan.
